channel.title: Comments for page "Some Are Born To Endless Night"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Some Are Born To Endless Night"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very well done. Could have been one of the best 001 proposals if you had gone that route. Leaves me a bit reminiscent at what could have been. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh. Well. That was just exquisite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Lot of Macbeth references here, right? With the "last syllable of recorded time"

Also take the +1 this is fantastically disturbing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since the authorship is no longer clear from this: this tale was written by me, Tufto, on my first account prior to my deleting it. You can find more of my stuff here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First of all, I have to say that this tale is amazing. It's definitely my favorite out of all that I've read, and it really deserves more recognition. Second, I've got some questions about how this fits in with the Scarlet King and Sarkic parts of the canon. Was it meant to have any sort of ties to those two entities? Is it implied that the darkness is the Scarlet King? And if these children are the ones creating the anomalies, where does that leave Yaldabaoth? Are they just one conglomerate entity that seeks to destroy our reality? Sorry about all the questions, but the tale was just so interesting and thought provoking that I need to know more

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
3434 targets exclusively Foundation personnel, as they are the ones who fight on in the dark.

And in this case, those children are not Foundation personnel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ho. Ly. Shit.

This is possibly one of the best tales I've read, almost topping Quiet Days. The idea that its not something, but someONE making the anomalies from within the first is something I've never considered.

And also the idea that they can feel regret for those anomalies makes it so much sadder…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…and after all this time, someone of us would rather remain in the darkness.

Wonder why you didn't reference that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep- the implication is that they're the source of all anomalies. This was a recycled 001 proposal, after all ;).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, does this mean that Jacob and the group of kids actually created a whole bunch of anomalies?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Purple is a pretty colour and you carried it off very well indeed. It makes it somewhat more surreal, and the self-awareness of it takes at least some of the edge off.

+1 for nightmare fuelling dream-prose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WOW that was a fun ride. +1 to you good sir

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a little purple, but my god. This was all kinds of satisfying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I really couldn't think of a way to expand upon the proposal; it really was just a tale with window-dressing.

I think I'll wait until I'm a more experienced skip writer before I try another 001 proposal. It might very well be linked to this article, though; I still think there's more to explore with the Dark and the disappearances.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unfortunate that you did not manage to find a way to rework it as an 001, but I suppose that not all things have to be known by the Foundation.

At least that allows your chance to develop your 001 and churn out your first Tale here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I think I'll have to comb through it and purge some of the flab later this week. It needs to be streamlined a bit more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm! Neat, although it did feel overwrought in points, but I can forgive that quite easily (dunno if others will).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My god is this one epic piece of writing. +1 to you and a shame it's all I can give as much this deserves so much more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was originally going to be a 001 proposal. I became disillusioned with that, so thought I'd publish the bulk of it as a tale instead. My first tale, in fact. (With that in mind, I hope I've done everything properly).

With thanks to the following:

SpookyPizza
Varaxous
MrWrong
halitherses

This tale contains lots of poetical references, and lots of crosslinks. I will remove any crosslinks at the request of the original authors.
